A

addBlueKey() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method adds one blue key count to Chip's inventory.
addEnemiesToGame(GameSystem) - Method in class GameMap2
This method is called after the map is loaded to add enemies to the game system.
addMicrochip() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method adds one microchip count to Chip's inventory.
addRedKey() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method adds one red key count to Chip's inventory.

BlankTile - Class in Unnamed Package
 
BlankTile() - Constructor for class BlankTile
Default constructor for BlankTile with no item.

Constructor for a BlankTile that holds an Item.

BlueDoor() - Constructor for class BlueDoor

C

canSwim() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method checks if Chip can swim (has flippers)
canWalkOnFire() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method checks if Chip can walk on fire (has fireboots)
changeChipPosition(int, int, char) - Method in class GameSystem
This method updates Chip's position and handles interaction with different map elements such as doors, tiles, exits, and more.
checkCollision(ChipPlayer) - Method in class NaiveEnemy
Checks if enemy collides with Chip.
checkMoveCondition(int, int) - Method in class GameSystem
This method checks whether Chip can move to the given coordinates based on the movement input.
chipMovement(char) - Method in class GameSystem
This method determines the new position of Chip based on the direction or input.It also checks whether the input/move is valid or not.
ChipPlayer - Class in Unnamed Package
 
ChipPlayer(int, int) - Constructor for class ChipPlayer
Constructor for ChipPlayer given the starting position in the map.
createBottomPanel(JLabel, JTextField, ChipPlayer) - Method in class Styling
Creates the bottom panel containing movement instructions, input field, and inventory button.
createHorizontalWall(int, int, int) - Method in class GameMap
This method creates a horizontal wall by filling a row with WallTile objects
createMapContainer(JPanel) - Method in class Styling
Wraps the game map panel inside a styled container with borders.
createTopPanel(JLabel, JLabel, int) - Method in class Styling
Creates and styles the top information panel that displays the level and microchip count.
createVerticalWall(int, int, int) - Method in class GameMap
THis method creates a vertical wall by filling a column with WallTile objects

D

Door - Class in Unnamed Package
The Door class represents a door on the GameMap.
Door() - Constructor for class Door
 

E

enterDoor(ChipPlayer) - Method in class BlueDoor
Checks if Chip have a blue key.
enterDoor(ChipPlayer) - Method in class Door
This method determines the condition/behavior when Chip tries to enter.
enterDoor(ChipPlayer) - Method in class RedDoor
This method checks if Chip have a red key.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class BlankTile
This method handles the condition when Chip enters the BlankTile.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class ExitTile
This method marks Chip as having entered/reached the ExitTile.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class FireTile
This method handles the logic/condition when Chip steps on a FireTile.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class ForceFloor
This method handles the prompt when Chip steps onto the ForceFloor
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class IceTile
This method handles the logic when Chip steps on an IceTile.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class TeleportTile
This method handles teleportation when Chip steps on the tile.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class Tile
This is an abstract method that must be implemented by its subclasses.
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class WallTile
This method handles the logic when Chip enters or walks through a tile
enterTile(ChipPlayer) - Method in class WaterTile
This method handles the logic/condition when Chip enters a WaterTile.
exitGame(JFrame) - Static method in class Main

ExitTile() - Constructor for class ExitTile

fillMap() - Method in class GameMap
This method fills the grid/map with blank tiles and called during map initialization.

G

GameMap - Class in Unnamed Package
The GameMap class represents the 2D map of Chip's Challenge.
GameMap() - Constructor for class GameMap
The constructor that initializes the map to a 20x20 grid (toned-down version), fills it with BlankTiles, and prepares the map elements that are specific for the different subclasses/maps.
GameMap1 - Class in Unnamed Package
The GameMap1 class represents the subclass of GameMap.
GameMap1() - Constructor for class GameMap1
 
GameMap2 - Class in Unnamed Package
The GameMap2 class represents the subclass of GameMap.
GameMap2() - Constructor for class GameMap2
 
GamePanel - Class in Unnamed Package
The GamePanel class is responsible for rendering the entire game world, including the map tiles, Chip, and all enemy entities.
GamePanel(GameMap, ChipPlayer, List<NaiveEnemy>) - Constructor for class GamePanel
Constructs a new GamePanel that displays the given map, the player character, and all active enemies.
GameSystem - Class in Unnamed Package
 
GameSystem() - Constructor for class GameSystem
Constructor that initializes the first level and sets up user input handling
getBlueKeyCount() - Method in class ChipPlayer
 
getEnemies() - Method in class GameSystem
Gets the list of enemies in the current level.
getFirebootsStatus() - Method in class ChipPlayer
 
getFlippersStatus() - Method in class ChipPlayer
 
getItem() - Method in class BlankTile
This method returns the item on top of the BlankTile.
getMap() - Method in class GameMap
This method returns the 2D map of Chip's challenge.
getMapLength() - Method in class GameMap
This method returns the length of the game map.
getMapWidth() - Method in class GameMap
This method returns the width of the game map.
getMicrochipCount() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method returns Chip's current microchip count.
getRedKeyCount() - Method in class ChipPlayer
 
getRequiredMicrochips() - Method in class GameMap
This method returns the required amount of microchips in the map
getSpawnX() - Method in class GameMap
This method returns Chip's spawn x coordinte
getSpawnY() - Method in class GameMap
This method returns Chip's spawn y coordinte
getSprite() - Method in class BlankTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class BlueDoor
Returns the sprite image used to represent this door.
getSprite() - Method in class BlueKey
Returns the sprite image used to represent this item.
getSprite() - Method in class ChipPlayer
Returns the sprite image used to represent chip.
getSprite() - Method in class Door
Abstract method that returns the sprite image used to represent this door.
getSprite() - Method in class ExitTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class FireBoots
Returns the sprite image used to represent this item.
getSprite() - Method in class FireTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class Flippers
Returns the sprite image used to represent this item.
getSprite() - Method in class ForceFloor
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class IceTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class Item
This is an abstract method that returns the sprite image used to represent this item.
getSprite() - Method in class MapElement
This is an abstract method that returns the sprite image used to represent different map elements.
getSprite() - Method in class Microchip
Returns the sprite image used to represent this item.
getSprite() - Method in class NaiveEnemy
 
getSprite() - Method in class RedDoor
Returns the sprite image used to represent this door.
getSprite() - Method in class RedKey
Returns the sprite image used to represent this item.
getSprite() - Method in class TeleportTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class Tile
This is an abstract method that returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class WallTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getSprite() - Method in class WaterTile
Returns the sprite image used to represent this tile.
getTargetX() - Method in class TeleportTile
Gets the target X coordinate for teleportation.
getTargetY() - Method in class TeleportTile
Gets the target Y coordinate for teleportation.
getTeleportId() - Method in class TeleportTile
Gets the teleport identifier for linking pairs.
getXDirection() - Method in class ForceFloor
This method returns the x coordinate direction of the ForceFloor.
getXPosition() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method returns Chip's current x position
getXPosition() - Method in class NaiveEnemy
 
getYDirection() - Method in class ForceFloor
This method returns the y coordinate direction of the ForceFloor.
getYPosition() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method returns Chip's current y position
getYPosition() - Method in class NaiveEnemy
 
giveFireboots() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method sets hasFireboots to true.
giveFlippers() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method sets hasFlippers to true.

H

handleTeleportation(TeleportTile) - Method in class GameSystem
Handles teleportation between linked teleport tiles.
hasBlueKey() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method checks if Chip has more than 0 blue key.
hasRedKey() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method checks if Chip has more than 0 red key.

isAtExit() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method checks if Chip is already at exit.
isChipAlive() - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method returns Chip's current life status
isLocked() - Method in class BlueDoor
This method checks if the current blue door is currently locked
isLocked() - Method in class Door
This method checks if the door is currently locked.
isLocked() - Method in class RedDoor
This method checks if current red door is currently locked
isWalkable() - Method in class ExitTile
This method determines if Chip can walk/pass through the ExitTile
isWalkable() - Method in class FireTile
This method etermines if Chip can walk on a FireTile.
isWalkable() - Method in class ForceFloor
This method determines if Chip can walk/pass through a ForceFloor
isWalkable() - Method in class IceTile
This method determines if Chip can walk on an IceTile.
isWalkable() - Method in class TeleportTile
This method determines if Chip can walk on a TeleportTile.
isWalkable() - Method in class Tile
This method checks whether the current tile is walkable or not.
isWalkable() - Method in class WallTile
This method checks if the tile is walkable.
isWalkable() - Method in class WaterTile
This method etermines if Chip can walk/pass through a WaterTile.

NaiveEnemy - Class in Unnamed Package

O

onPickup(ChipPlayer) - Method in class BlueKey
This method handles operation when Chip picks up this blue key.
onPickup(ChipPlayer) - Method in class FireBoots
This method handles the behavior/logic when Chip picks up fireboots
onPickup(ChipPlayer) - Method in class Flippers
This method handles the behavior/logic when Chip picks up flippers.
onPickup(ChipPlayer) - Method in class Item
This is an abstract method that determines what happens when a certain item is collected by Chip (keys to unlock doors, boots to allow walking over specific tiles, etc.)
onPickup(ChipPlayer) - Method in class Microchip
This method determines what happens when Chip picks up a microchip.
onPickup(ChipPlayer) - Method in class RedKey
This method handles operation when Chip picks up this red key.

P

paintComponent(Graphics) - Method in class GamePanel
Renders the entire game screen, drawing each map tile, items on tiles, the Chip player, and all enemies.
prepareMapElements() - Method in class GameMap
This abstract method that must be implemented by subclasses/specific maps to define specific map layouts, item placement, etc.
prepareMapElements() - Method in class GameMap1
This method prepares the map elements, item and tile distribution for map 1 of Chip's Challenge
prepareMapElements() - Method in class GameMap2
This method prepares the map elements, item and tile distribution for map 2 of Chip's Challenge

resetToMainMenu() - Method in class GameSystem
 

S

setAtExit(boolean) - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method sets Chip's atExit status to the boolean value passed in.
setChipAlive(boolean) - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method sets Chip's alive status.
setPlayerSpawn(int, int) - Method in class GameMap
This method sets Chip's starting/spawn position in the map
setRequiredMicrochips(int) - Method in class GameMap
This method sets the required number of microchips for Chip to win the current level
setXPosition(int) - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method sets Chip's x position to the value passed in
setXPosition(int) - Method in class NaiveEnemy
 
setYPosition(int) - Method in class ChipPlayer
This method sets Chip's y position to the value passed in
setYPosition(int) - Method in class NaiveEnemy
 
showInventory(ChipPlayer) - Method in class Styling
Displays the player's inventory in a styled pop-up window.
showMainMenu() - Method in class Styling
Shows the game's main menu screen with title and play/exit buttons.
slideOnForceFloor(ForceFloor) - Method in class GameSystem
This method handles Chip's sliding when stepping on a Force Floor on the map.
slideOnIce(char) - Method in class GameSystem
Handles Chip's sliding on ice tiles.
startGame() - Method in class GameSystem
This method starts the game loop where the player can move around, view inventory, or exit their current game.
startGame(JFrame) - Static method in class Main
 
styleButton(JButton) - Method in class Styling
Styles a button consistently with the game's design: hover color change, border, sizing, and hand cursor.
styleExitCurrent(JFrame, GameSystem, JTextField) - Method in class Styling
Displays a confirmation popup asking the user whether they want to exit the current game.
styleGameSystemFormat(JFrame) - Method in class Styling
Applies standard formatting for the main game window.
Styling - Class in Unnamed Package
The Styling class centralizes all UI styling, formatting, and popup window creation for Chip's Challenge.
Styling() - Constructor for class Styling

This method decrements Chip's blue key count by one after usage.
useRedKey() - Method in class ChipPlayer
